title: Telephone Facilities - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Telephone Facilities 14 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Homeless Families Homeless Youth Pregnant Individuals Veteran/Military Personnel Issues CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH OUTREACH CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Walk-in center providing referrals, food, clothing, DME, camping basic needs, and water for anyone experiencing homelessness or extremely low-income. Conducts outreach to veterans and non-veterans living in homeless camps by providing potable water, food, clothing, phone/fax services,gas vouchers (once per year), ordering DD214 documents for veterans, and facilitating connection to veterans services. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, who need to be screened for eligibility for programs and connection to services for housing, medical, employment and more. Street Outreach services are available for veteran and non-veteran homeless and low-income individuals and households. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3:45pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIACARE SERVICES VETERANS RESOURCE CENTER VETERAN RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides hygiene items, a temproary mailing address, telephone and computer use, housing assistance, case management services, and information and referral to other community partners. 601 N Grape Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Veterans and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 779-8564 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV DAY CENTER HIV SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center serving hot breakfast and lunches, access to phones and computers, and recreational activities such as art therapy, games, and video-based media. Also provides clothing, showers, mail services, and hygiene supplies. Guests have access to laundry services, massages, acupuncture, foot care, and haircuts on a sign-up basis. 2941 NE Ainsworth Street Basement Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Individuals who are HIV-positive and have incomes no greater than 300% of the federal poverty level Hours: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday 1pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 460-3822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, clothing, laundry, personal hygiene items, showers, job training classes, mentoring programs, referrals to other resources, and access to telephone and computer services. For the winter season, offering tents, sleeping bags, and blankets. 834 Monroe Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 with a focus on individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 11am-6:30pm, Friday 11am-3pm Lunch: noon-2pm Dinner: 5pm Email (541) 344-5583 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER STREET LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term overnight shelter, two meals per day, and cleanup facilities for 30 homeless and street dependent youth attempting to make changes in their lives. Youth may access clothing, medical assistance, and employment education support. When severe weather is declared by the city, 5 additional beds will open. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Displaced youth ages 16-24. Youth must be working with a case manager on a plan in order to access a bed on a continuing basis. Hours: 7 days per week 9pm-9am Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOIN JOIN PDX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center. Services include access to showers, bathrooms, mail pick up and PO Box setup, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, on-site WiFi and telephone usage, and specialized information and referral. 1435 NE 81st Avenue Suite 100 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Unrestricted with priority given to homeless individuals or families Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 9am-5pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed on national holidays. Email (503) 232-2031 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEBOAT SERVICES DAYTIME NAVIGATION SHELTER LIFEBOAT DAY SHELTER AND NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center with access to socks, laundry facilities, Wi-Fi, computer and telephone access, fax, coffee, snacks, a clothing closet, assistance in navigating social services, and specialized information and referral. Camping gear may be available, depending on donations. 1076 Franklin Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 6am-6pm, Thursday/Friday 8am-6pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with laundry facilities, phone use, temporary mailing address, showers, and daily meals. Also provides street outreach, case management, mental health counseling, education, and assistance finding housing. Eligible youth will be added to a central housing referral list for all programs they qualify for. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: -Drop-in Center: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm -Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am-9:30am -Lunch: 7 days per week noon-1pm -Snack: 7 days per week 3pm-3:30pm -Dinner: 7 days per week 4pm-5pm Email (541) 686-4310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHRA OHRA COMMUNITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing services, case management, information and referral, mailing address, telephone, benefits and services assistance, job search-related printing, resume creation, hygiene supplies, and education opportunities. Showers and laundry are available twice a week. Each individual who showers can do a small load of laundry. 2350 Ashland Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers and Laundry: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm. First come, first serve. Email (541) 631-2235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPEN DOOR HOUSINGWORKS UNHOUSED DAYTIME ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center with access to hot meals, food boxes, showers, personal hygiene items, telephones, mail services, and case management support. 34420 SW Tualatin Valley Highway Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Food Boxes: Unrestricted Homeless services: Income must be at or below 50% Area Medan Income (AMI) Other services: Individuals experiencing or at-risk of homelessness Hours: Day center services: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm Hot meals: Monday-Friday noon-1pm Food boxes: Monday-Friday 9am-2:30pm Email (503) 640-6689 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A drop in center that provides basic needs services such as meals, recreation/activities, showers, laundry, phone, mailing address, and computers. Also offers a safe space for LGBTQ youth. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are experiencing homelessness and are eligible to enter the Homeless Youth Continuum Hours: Drop-In: Tuesday/Thursday/Sunday 1pm-6pm Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10am Lunch: Sunday-Friday 1pm-2pm Dinner: Sunday-Friday 5pm-6pm Email (503) 535-3800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center. Services include laundry facilities, showers, meals, phone access, voicemail services, clothing, blankets, personal care supplies, job search assistance, and information and referral to other services. Visitors can use indoor, climate-controlled seating and outdoor, shaded picnic tables. 450 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income and homeless adults without minor children Hours: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am Lunch: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday noon, Wednesday 11am Showers and laundry: 7 days per week 8:30am-noon and 2pm-5pm Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center with laundry, showers, internet access, mail services, clothing vouchers, on-site clothing, and hygiene supplies. Assistance in obtaining identification documents. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 and pregnant women (proof of pregnancy required) who are experiencing homelessness Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE FATHER'S HEART Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in day center with services that include two meals a day with a sack lunch to-go, showers by appointment (not wheelchair accessible), haircuts, computer and telephone access, blankets, clothing, shoes, and hygiene items. Referrals to resources surrounding housing, mental health, and substance abuse. Assistance with the determination of homelessness letters to obtain an Oregon ID. 603 12th Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Homeless adults age 18 and older and adults with families Hours: Day Center and meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Email (503) 722-9780 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH OUTREACH CENTER COLUMBIACARE SERVICES VETERAN RESOURCE CENTER ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV SERVICES HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS STREET LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER JOIN JOIN PDX LIFEBOAT SERVICES LIFEBOAT DAY SHELTER AND NAVIGATION CENTER LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER OHRA OHRA COMMUNITY RESOURCE CENTER OPEN DOOR HOUSINGWORKS UNHOUSED DAYTIME ACCESS CENTER OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER THE FATHER'S HEART THE FATHER'S HEART
